data.title: Lloyds: Strong Profitability and 20% ROTE Support Hold Rating Amid Limited Upside and Political Uncertainty
data.description:
Lloyds Banking, the Financial sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Perlie Mong from Bank of America Securities reiterated a Hold rating on the stock and has a p130.00 price target.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Perlie Mong has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, balancing Lloyds’ strong profitability with constraints on further upside. Lloyds is expected to deliver a sustainable return on tangible equity near 20%, supported by hedge benefits that should cushion the impact of future rate cuts and competitive pressure on margins.

However, much of this strength is already reflected in consensus expectations and the current valuation, which limits re-rating potential. While non-interest income and areas like CIB and retail wealth offer credible growth avenues, Lloyds’ position as the largest UK retail bank makes materially above-market balance sheet growth harder to achieve, and a fluid political backdrop adds further uncertainty.

data.title: Molecular Partners Reaffirmed at Buy as Analyst Cites MP0712 Progress and Keeps $12 Price Target Unchanged
data.description:
Molecular Partners, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Charles Zhu from LifeSci Capital maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a $12.00 price target.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Charles Zhu has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including encouraging early clinical signs from MP0712 in small cell lung cancer and strong execution by Molecular Partners’ management team. The dose-escalation study is progressing with repeat dosing in the first cohort, and observed hematologic effects so far align with the expected dip-and-recovery profile, supporting continued development.

Zhu also highlights MP0712’s competitive positioning as a DLL3-targeted radioligand, noting its isotope-agnostic DARPin design and the exit of a key rival from the DLL3 radioligand space. Management’s ambitious efficacy goals, upcoming higher-dose data readouts by ASCO 2027, and a solid cash runway of roughly 1.6 years underpin his conviction, and the price target remains unchanged at $12, reinforcing the favorable risk-reward outlook.

Zhu covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Revolution Medicines, Kura Oncology, and IDEAYA Biosciences. According to TipRanks, Zhu has an average return of 26.4% and a 62.04% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on BrainsWay, Citing Strong Deep TMS Data and SWIFT Coverage Expansion; 12-Month Price Target Maintained at $17
data.description:
Ram Selvaraju, an analyst from H.C. Wainwright, reiterated the Buy rating on Brainsway. The associated price target remains the same with $17.00.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Ram Selvaraju has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to BrainsWay’s Deep TMS platform and expanding reimbursement traction. He highlights robust real-world clinical data in comorbid PTSD and MDD, where large-scale retrospective results showed strong response and remission rates, reinforcing the therapy’s value proposition and likely strengthening prescriber and payer confidence in adoption.

Ram Selvaraju’s rating is based on the view that growing U.S. coverage for the SWIFT accelerated Deep TMS protocol and related indications should support meaningful long-term growth. He underscores SWIFT as a key near-term catalyst, noting its ability to condense treatment schedules, enhance clinic capacity, and potentially lower payer costs, all while maintaining existing reimbursement economics, and therefore reiterates his unchanged 12‑month price target of $17.

data.title: Galderma Buy Rating Reiterated as Nemluvio Momentum Offsets RelabotulinumtoxinA Delay; 12‑Month Price Target Raised to CHF260
data.description:
Analyst Ram Selvaraju of H.C. Wainwright reiterated a Buy rating on Galderma Group AG, boosting the price target to CHF260.00.

4th of July Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Ram Selvaraju has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including his view that the FDA’s Complete Response Letter for RelabotulinumtoxinA reflects fixable manufacturing and analytical issues rather than problems with safety or efficacy. He expects Galderma to address the FDA’s observations promptly, limiting any U.S. launch delay to under a year, while approvals and launches in 33 non‑U.S. markets continue to progress with encouraging initial demand trends.

Ram Selvaraju’s rating is based on confidence that Galderma’s broader therapeutic dermatology franchise, led by Nemluvio, can offset temporary headwinds from the RelabotulinumtoxinA setback. Nemluvio is already showing robust sales growth, particularly in the U.S. and in atopic dermatitis, and he believes stronger‑than‑expected uptake can fill any near‑term revenue gap, supporting his reiterated Buy recommendation and increased 12‑month price target of CHF260.

According to TipRanks, Selvaraju is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 25.8% and a 63.40% success rate. Selvaraju covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Cognition Therapeutics, Urogen Pharma, and LB Pharmaceuticals, Inc..

In another report released yesterday, Barclays also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a CHF200.00 price target.
